title: ~530 ruff findings in src/ (pre-existing); CI lint is advisory only
observation: ruff check src reports ~530 findings (213 E402, 177 F401, 72 UP035, 27 B033, 15 B904, ...); CI ruff has continue-on-error: true. The 0.06 trees pass clean.
interpretation: Largely benign legacy style debt, but non-blocking CI lint lets regressions land unseen.
evidence: ruff histogram (log #4); ci.yml:30; new-tree ruff pass (#5)
recommendation: Per-module burn-down + per-file-ignores for intentional re-exports; flip CI ruff to blocking on cleaned trees.

title: Novelty-weighting uses inconsistent representative keys (string vs int sort) -> novelty defaults to 1.0 for some stories
observation: novelty_of keyed by sorted(members,key=int)[0] but read via sorted(members)[0]; for ids like {2,11} the reps differ, lookup misses, novelty defaults to 1.0.
interpretation: Opt-in novelty view mis-scores some multi-source echoes as fully novel; default path unaffected.
evidence: repro (log #14): sorted({'2','11'})[0]=='11' vs key=int -> '2', mismatch True
root_cause: Two sorted() calls compute the story representative with different keys; test ids 1..40 coincided so it slipped through.
recommendation: Compute rep once (min(members,key=int)) and reuse in both loops; add a regression test with ids like {2,11}.

title: Dead legacy scraper: tests hit the real network, and its robots check fails OPEN
observation: Legacy Scraper has no live references; two tests call scrape_all_sources() unmocked -> real DNS to test-source-1/2.com; robots check returns True on fetch error.
interpretation: Both confined to dead code: tests reach the network (flaky/slow offline); legacy path fails OPEN on robots, contradicting the fail-closed value (live path is correct).
evidence: errors.log (#8); no live refs (#10); scraper.py:174 (#11); test lines (#9)
root_cause: Superseded module kept only for its tests, which were never network-isolated.
recommendation: Quarantine the legacy scraper or mock the fetch in those two tests; note the fail-open robots if kept.

title: Tests are not hermetic: they pollute working-tree data/ and audit/; app DB engine is a shared singleton
location: data/ (open_omniscience.db 610KB, custody_log.db, keys/, briefing_cache.json); app-level tests
observation: After a run, repo data/ holds a seeded DB, custody DB, keys, cache; app DB engine is bound at import so per-test OO_DATA_DIR does not always re-bind; surfaced as order-dependent test_law_api counts.
interpretation: Shared on-disk state makes outcomes order-dependent; artifacts are git-ignored so no repo leakage (caps severity).
evidence: ls data/ + git-ignored (#7); law_api total-vs-created fix
root_cause: Module-level engine bound at import + no isolating fixture for app tests.
recommendation: Autouse fixture forcing OO_DATA_DIR/tmp + OO_NO_SCHEDULER; lazy/rebindable engine; assert no writes under repo data/.

title: Briefing recompute runs the MinHash/LSH near-dup pass 2-3x over overlapping sets
observation: echo_chamber (corpus_actors) and lonely_signal (story_prominence) each run a near-dup pass over the same recent news; model_legislation runs a third over law docs.
interpretation: Redundant CPU per refresh; bounded (<=2000 articles) and cached on the scheduler so no user latency -> Low.
root_cause: Independent producers re-derive the same graph; no per-refresh memo.
recommendation: Compute the corpus near-dup/actor graph once per refresh and share it across producers.

title: Post-quantum (ML-DSA) hybrid-signature path is unexercised in this environment
observation: pqcrypto absent -> 6 skips; annotation bundles signed/verified Ed25519-only; hybrid branch not run.
interpretation: Not a defect (designed to degrade + label honestly); the hybrid/PQC claim is just unverified here.
recommendation: Add a CI lane with the [pqc] extra to exercise the hybrid sign/verify + a hybrid bundle round-trip; note env-dependence in docs.

title: README seed-count understates the default first-run seed
observation: README says ~1,780 sources, but default seed now also includes spectrum (278), markets, and legal (51). sources.yml has 1905 raw entries.
interpretation: Minor downward drift, not a false claim; low user impact.
evidence: grep claims vs grep -c name: counts (#13)
root_cause: README not updated when extra catalogs were folded into the default seed.
recommendation: Update README to ~2,100+ across news, markets, spectrum and law/IP.

title: Positive controls confirmed (secrets, ignores, quarantine, docs, verifier, timestamps, migration)
observation: No hardcoded secrets in live src; data/audit/logs/*.key/*.db git-ignored; quarantine not imported by live code; all 12 in-app docs exist; verify_evidence.py runs; datetime.utcnow()=0; alembic upgrade head clean; 691 passed.
interpretation: Stated safety posture is borne out by direct checks; recorded for balance.
recommendation: Keep as CI invariants (secret scan, ignore-list check, quarantine-not-imported assertion, doc-existence test).

title: Redundant resolve_keyword() calls in some briefing producers
observation: emotion_profile_card resolves the same term up to 3x; other producers resolve twice.
interpretation: Correct results, redundant DB lookups; negligible cost.
root_cause: Inline ternary re-evaluates resolve_keyword instead of binding once.
recommendation: Bind kw once and reuse; guard on None.

title: Curated deep consolidated-law URLs are not live-verified (network off)
observation: The trackable documents: use specific deep URLs; none were fetched (network off), so liveness is unverified.
interpretation: A moved URL makes a document untrackable; failure mode is SAFE (records fetch error, no fabrication) but tracking yields nothing until fixed.
evidence: read of legal_sources.yml + track_document error handling; not executed
root_cause: Deep links are more fragile than landing/ELI URLs.
recommendation: With network enabled, run a one-off liveness check; prefer stable ELI/landing URLs; surface never-reached-baseline docs in the UI.
